codeReview.summary: Frontend implementation is complete with admin content management pages for subjects, topics, lessons, questions, and test-series. However, the backend API endpoints that the frontend calls do not exist - /admin/subjects, /admin/topics, /admin/lessons, /admin/questions, /admin/test-series are not defined in the backend router. The frontend will fail at runtime when trying to perform any CRUD operations.

issues.description: Backend admin router does not implement content management endpoints. Frontend calls /admin/subjects, /admin/topics, /admin/lessons, /admin/questions, /admin/test-series but these are not defined. Existing endpoints are only: /admin/login, /admin/dashboard, /admin/users, /admin/users/{id}, /admin/users/{id}/status
No Pydantic schemas defined for AdminSubject, AdminTopic, AdminLesson, AdminQuestion, AdminTestSeries, or their Create/Update DTOs in the admin schemas module
No service methods defined for content management CRUD operations (create_subject, create_topic, create_lesson, create_question, create_test_series, etc.)

Inconsistent options parsing: openEditModal parses options with JSON.parse (line 97), but the table display on line 290 directly renders question.options without parsing, which would show [object Object] or similar if options is a JSON string

Question options parsing in openEditModal uses typeof check 'string' but the adminApi types define options as string[] - if the API returns a string (JSON serialized), the parse might fail silently. Consider using try/catch with fallback.

File upload via handleFileUpload embeds images as markdown directly into content field, which may not render properly depending on how content is displayed. Consider using a proper rich text renderer or sanitizing content.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Frontend skill procedure requires running agent-browser for UI validation after implementation, but this step appears not to have been performed (no browser verification in the synthesis). The synthesis indicates validators failed due to environment issues (database not running, jest/vitest mismatch), not code issues, but a full UI verification was skipped.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill SKILL.md states: 'Open in browser via agent-browser and verify: Page renders without errors, All interactive elements work, Mobile viewport (375px) displays correctly, No console errors'. The validation synthesis shows only test/typecheck/lint commands were run.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend calls /admin/upload endpoint for MinIO file uploads, but services.yaml does not document MinIO startup command. services.yaml has postgres, redis, minio start commands but the minio healthcheck uses port 3102.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: adminApi.uploadFile in admin.ts calls POST /admin/upload with FormData. The feature description mentions 'File upload API for lesson images/videos via MinIO' but no /admin/upload endpoint exists in router.py.

summary: FAIL - Backend API endpoints missing. Frontend has complete implementation for admin content management UI (subjects, topics, lessons, questions, test-series CRUD, bulk import, file upload) but the corresponding backend endpoints do not exist. The AdminService, schemas, and router endpoints for content management were not implemented. This is a full-stack feature that requires both frontend and backend to work.
